t_ch: 我就想知道知道。
t_en: I just wanna know, though.

t_en: Remember that Abelane Galaxy Lottery I was telling you about? It doesn't matter if you don't. All you need to know is that it's a random number lottery where one person can hit the jackpot. It's a big, happy event.

t_ch: 而「无罪大乐透」则是同一个星系的监狱里举办的盛事。他们会抽选一个犯人暂获一日自由，其他的犯人得在那一天替他把苦役干了。
t_en: Well, there's also an Innocence Lottery over in the same star system — in their prisons. The prize draw offers one prisoner one day of freedom, and all the other prisoners have to pick up their hard labor for the day.

t_ch: 某位不愿意透露姓名的老板要我帮他预测出这个「天选之子」。
t_en: A manager who wishes to remain anonymous, wants me to help him predict the Chosen One for this lottery, now.

t_ch: 释放罪犯？会乱套吧？

t_ch: 为短生种的事操心可不值得。何况这乐透的结果就像是一日假释罢了。
t_en: It's not worth worrying about that for a short-life species like yourself. Besides, the prize is just one single day of liberation.

t_ch: 您只管启动阵基，别担心。咱们既不会干涉那地方的司法，也不会让罪犯逍遥法外。
t_en: Just focus on starting the base terminal up, and don't worry about it. We're not interfering with their justice system, and we're not sending any grievous criminal on the run permanently.

t_ch: 是犯人请求你预测结果吗？
t_en: Is it a prisoner who's asking you to make this prediction?

t_ch: 不，付钱的是刑事犯受害者家属互助会的人。
t_en: Nope, this commission's from the Relatives of Criminal Victims Mutual Aid Society.

t_ch: 他们付上这笔钱，是为了知道究竟谁会被放出监狱，好早做准备……

t_ch: 我们的占算结果只是让人提早知道罢了，其实咱们什么也没有改变。
t_en: Our results just give people advanced notice about events. In fact, we don't actually change anything.

t_ch: 好吧，我就不过问了。
t_en: Alright, I won't ask.

t_en: Good job, {NICKNAME}. Results obtained.

t_ch: 唔，我手头确实有桩必须要占算的事情，走吧。
t_en: Well, I just so happen to have something that needs divining. Let's go.

t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 你自己？

t_en: Haha, it's exactly because I can't hide things for much longer that I need to borrow the matrix for a quick divination.

t_ch: 很好，接下来还有一个阵法要算，走吧。

t_ch: 走吧。

t_ch: 稍等，我还有点事。

t_ch: 有什么事情快要暴露了吧。

t_ch: 你已经猜到一二了……

t_ch: 是啊，我之前应该讲的很明白了吧…

t_ch: 我真的不知道你在做什么……

t_ch: 我不会做这种事。

t_ch: 我就知道，咱俩可是好朋友啊。

t_ch: 那得看我的心情。

t_ch: 喂，这玩笑可开的不太妙啊。那绘星在此祝你每天都有好心情吧。
t_en: Hey, that's not funny. Well then, wishing you a good mood every day!

t_ch: 我可不能保证。

t_ch: 喂，这玩笑可开的不太妙啊。这一路来我可从没短了你的酬劳。你瞧，下次若有类似的活计，我一定会拉上你的。

t_ch: 走吧。
t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 你在忙什么呢？

t_en: Let's keep running our divinations.

t_ch: 随绘星一同前往了穷观阵。

t_ch: 关于例行占算

t_ch: 喂，算你幸运，今天有幸得到像我这样的大师指点。
t_en: Hey, consider yourself lucky today to have received guidance from a master like me.

t_en: Who am I? How can you not recognize me? Who are YOU?

t_ch: 大师，您不认识我了吗？
t_en: Master, do you not recognize me?

t_ch: 大师我贵人多忘事，不认识你也是正常的。
t_en: Busy people have short memories. It shouldn't come as a surprise that I can't seem to recall who you are.

t_ch: 咦，你怎么看起来这么眼熟。

t_ch: 狡猾的人类，口是心非。
t_en: Slippery, duplicitous human.

t_ch: 现在没空陪你玩。
t_en: I don't have time to play with you right now

t_ch: 你会回来找我的，因为你想和我下棋！
t_en: You'll be back! Because I KNOW you want to face me in chess!

t_ch: 废话少说，上手见真章。
t_en: Silence. Spare me your words and show me your skill.

t_ch: 你别走，你再来一盘…
t_en: Wait, don't leave. Another round please...

t_ch: 你先走吧，我再想想…

t_ch: 高手就是寂寞啊……

t_ch: 嗯，你的水平勉强还可以。当然了，比我差远了。
t_en: Hm. Your skill is acceptable. Nothing comparable to mine, of course.

t_ch: 有没有难一点的？
t_en: Got anything harder?

t_ch: 嘿，夸你一句你还得瑟上了。
t_en: Oh. Turned cocky from a single bit of praise, have we?

t_ch: 多谢大师指导。
t_en: Thanks for your guidance, master.

t_ch: 不错不错，这才是学习的态度。

t_en: Yes. Let's play right now.

t_en: I don't have time to play with you right now

t_en: You'll be back! Because I KNOW you want to face me in chess!

t_ch: 来吧。
t_en: Come on then.

t_ch: 那就开始吧。
t_en: Then let's start.

t_ch: 等一下。

t_en: Then I definitely won't lose.

t_ch: 你猜我会不会输？
t_en: Wanna guess whether I'll lose?

t_ch: …可恶。
t_en: ...Darn you.

t_ch: 少得意忘形了，解出这个棋局的人可是寥寥无几！
t_en: Stop gloating. Very few people can solve this chess puzzle!

t_ch: 来吧。
t_en: Come on then.

t_ch: 那就开始吧。
t_en: Then let's start.

t_ch: 等一下。
t_en: Wait a moment.

t_ch: 别告诉我你害怕了。
t_en: Don't tell me you're scared.

t_ch: 我懂了我懂了。
t_en: I get it. I really do.

t_ch: 懂了什么？
t_en: Get what?

t_ch: 你在说什么？
t_en: What are you talking about?

t_ch: 你一定是在扮猪吃老虎！既然如此，那我也就不藏着掖着了，准备迎接最后的挑战吧！
t_en: You're pretending to be weak so I lower my guard! In that case, I will no longer conceal my own potential. Prepare to face the ultimate, final challenge!

t_ch: 前所未有的复杂棋局，从未公开的独家设计！
t_en: A chess match of never-before-seen complexity, an exclusive design previously unrevealed!

t_ch: 一如既往的惨烈失败。
t_en: Another bitter defeat is coming, just like it always has.

t_ch: 最后一题了，快点吧。
t_en: Last question. Hurry it up.

t_ch: 话不多说，来吧！
t_en: Then enough talk. Have at you!

t_ch: 来吧。
t_en: Come on then.

t_ch: 那就开始吧。
t_en: Then let's start.

t_ch: 等一下。
t_en: Wait a moment.

t_ch: 都最后一题了，你还要打断一下吗？快点回来。
t_en: It's the last puzzle, and you still need to interrupt me? Hurry and come back soon.

t_ch: 你怎么了？
t_en: What's wrong?

t_ch: 棋鬼，你说话呀。
t_en: Chess Ghost, say something.

t_ch: 我败了…我彻彻底底地败了。我的人生…哦不，我的岁阳生活已经失去了意义。
t_en: I've been defeated... I've been completely and utterly defeated. My life... No, my existence as a heliobus has lost all meaning.

t_ch: 你出的棋局也蛮有挑战的。
t_en: Your chess puzzles are pretty challenging, actually.

t_ch: 败了就是败了，说再多也没有用。
t_en: Losing is losing. Words can't change that.

t_ch: 所以，要开始战斗了吗？
t_en: So, are you ready for another round?

t_ch: 再也不会了。作为棋手，请你让我保持基本的风度。
t_en: No. Never again. As a fellow chess player, please leave me with some basic dignity.

t_ch: 让棋鬼一个岁阳独自静静。
t_en: Let the Chess Ghost have some alone time for a bit.

t_ch: 你已经进步很多啦。
t_en: You've already improved a lot.

t_ch: 哼哼，别想讨好我！我要回去闭关修炼，下次我可不会手软！
t_en: Hmph. Don't try to butter me up! I must seclude myself and improve. I will not be so merciful next time!

t_ch: 你可以附身在聪明人身上。
t_en: You can possess someone smart.

t_ch: 好主意！
t_en: That's a good idea!

t_ch: 不对！我不是被十王司镇伏了吗？我怎么附身到聪明人身上。
t_en: Wait, no! I've been suppressed by the Ten-Lords Commission. How am I supposed to possess someone smart?

t_ch: 不对不对不对！你是在说我不够聪明？
t_en: Wait, no, no, no! Are you saying I'm not smart enough?

t_ch: 你走吧，我要回去生闷气了，要是再有下次，我可不会手软！
t_en: Leave. I wish to head back and sulk. If there is a next time, I will not be merciful!

t_ch: <size=28>{NICKNAME}大人请留步！</size>
t_en: <size=28>{NICKNAME}, please wait a moment!</size>

t_ch: 哎呦，盼星星盼月亮，总算把您给盼来了。那你看，咱们什么时候开始？
t_en: You're finally here! Oh, you don't know how long I've been waiting for this! So, when shall we start?

t_ch: 不是，你是谁啊…
t_en: Huh? Who are you?

t_ch: 我？一个默默无闻的「寻径指津」爱好者罢了。而您就不一样了，您可是我们「寻圈」的传奇人物。
t_en: Me? I'm just your average "Pathfinder" enthusiast. My name won't ring a bell with most people. You, on the other hand, are a legend in the community!

t_ch: 你怎么认识我？
t_en: You know me?

t_ch: 您是我们「寻圈」的大名人，哪一个玩「寻径指津」的认不得您的脸？
t_en: You're a big name in the community. Everyone that plays "Pathfinder" recognizes you.

t_ch: 开始，开什么始？
t_en: Start? Start what?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}大人说笑了，当然是「寻径指津」啦，整个仙舟还有比您更会玩这个游戏的人么？
t_en: Oh, you're such a jokester, {NICKNAME}. I'm talking about "Pathfinder," of course! Nobody else in the Xianzhou plays it better than you, after all.

t_ch: 等等，太卜司那个家伙…不会还没跟您说过咱们的活动吧？
t_en: Wait... Don't tell me that guy from the Divination Commission didn't tell you about our event?

t_ch: 这家伙，棋艺稀烂也就算了，一点小事都办不好，回头把他从「寻圈」开除。
t_en: Sheesh, I gave him a pass for being bad at chess, but he can't even do this simple task, right? I'm kicking him from the community when I get back.

t_ch: 他说过，但我忘了。
t_en: He told me, but I forgot about it.

t_ch: 他什么都没跟我说。
t_en: He didn't tell me anything.

t_ch: 你们这个圈子还搞霸凌呢。
t_en: You folks bully people in your community?

t_ch: 让您看笑话了，我再重新给您介绍下这次活动好了。
t_en: My apologies for the mix-up. As amends, why don't I introduce the event to you again?

t_ch: 虽然咱们圈内人都认为「寻径指津」天下第一，但放眼整个游戏市场，「寻径指津」还是小众游戏，您知道为什么吗？
t_en: Those of us in the community may think the world of "Pathfinder" as a game, but if you look at the gaming market as a whole, you'll find that "Pathfinder" ultimately remains a niche game. Why do you think that is?

t_ch: 因为太简单了。
t_en: Because it's too easy.

t_ch: 不可能！难道「枘凿六合」难度高了？玩家照样比咱们多。
t_en: Impossible! Did Hexanexus raise their difficulty? They're attracting more players than us.

t_ch: 因为圈子素质差。
t_en: Because the community atmosphere is poor.

t_ch: 咱这是益智游戏。人不都说吗——「智商高的人情商都低」。我们是高智商游戏圈子，素质当然差了。
t_en: This is a mind-training game. People always say, "Those with high IQs have low EQs." We are a high-IQ gaming community, so of course the atmosphere would suck.

t_ch: 因为这届玩家不行。
t_en: The players suck at the game.

t_ch: 这话可不敢乱说！还是要多在自己身上找原因，不能做不出好游戏就怪玩家！
t_en: You shouldn't make such a bold claim! It's important to reflect on ourselves more and not blame players when we aren't making good games!

t_ch: 关键是咱们「寻圈」缺乏意见领袖啊。你看看「帝垣琼玉」，人家有青雀——打工人的学习榜样。
t_en: But the crux of the problem is that the community lacks an opinion leader. If you look at Celestial Jade, you'll see that Qingque is their role model for the average person working a wage.

t_ch: 再看看「以太战线」，人家有银狼。那可是上了公司通缉令的人物，全银河无人不知无人不晓，虽然不是啥好名声。
t_en: What about Aetherium Wars? Well, they have Silver Wolf. As someone wanted by the IPC, her name is known across the cosmos — even if it's in infamy.

t_ch: 咱们「寻径指津」呢，一个拿得出手的知名玩家都没有，除了……
t_en: But "Pathfinder"? We don't even have a single prominent player to our name, except for...

t_ch: 除了棋鬼。
t_en: ...The Chess Ghost.

t_ch: 岁阳就算了吧！再把十王司的铁拳给引来……
t_en: Heliobi? Just forget about 'em! Don't let the iron fist of the Ten-Lords Commission chew us out again...

t_ch: 除了你。
t_en: ...You.

t_ch: 我？哈哈，我连个正式名字都不配有。
t_en: Me? Haha, I'm not even important enough to have an actual name.

t_ch: 除了我。
t_en: Except me.

t_ch: 就喜欢您这样有自知之明的天才。
t_en: I like geniuses with self-awareness like you.

t_ch: 就是您啊！勇往直前的开拓者、做好事不留名的无名客、名震银河的球棒侠、城市卫生的清道夫！
t_en: It's you! The fearless Trailblazer, the fame-forgoing altruistic Nameless, the batter who shakes the cosmos, and the custodian of urban cleanliness!

t_ch: 总之您就是咱们破圈的希望，只要您能在车水马龙的竞锋舰玩「寻径指津」，一定能引起很多人对这款游戏的关注。
t_en: In short, you're the "Pathfinder" community's only hope. If you play Pathfinder in a busy place like the Skysplitter, you're sure to draw attention to the game.

t_ch: 对不起，我比赛要开始了。
t_en: Sorry, but my match is about to start.

t_ch: 这么多人，好丢脸哦。
t_en: It's too embarrassing to play in front of so many people.

t_ch: 对我有什么好处。
t_en: What's in it for me?

t_ch: 别急，您请看这边！
t_en: Wait, don't be so hasty. Now, if you'll look over here...

t_ch: 一点心意不成敬意，事成之后这些都是您的。
t_en: It isn't much, but they're all yours once the deed is done.

t_ch: 愣着干啥，赶紧开始吧。
t_en: What are we waiting for? Let's get started.

t_ch: 好歹毒的招数，我玩还不行么。
t_en: What a ruthless gambit. I can't say no now, can I?

t_ch: 你准备好了就跟我说一声，我帮你摆棋盘。
t_en: Let me know when you're ready. I'll set up the chessboard for you.

t_ch: 我准备好了。
t_en: I'm ready.

t_ch: 我再准备下。
t_en: I need a moment to get ready.

t_ch: 我和宝箱在这等您。
t_en: I'll be waiting for you here with the chest.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}大人，要继续玩「寻径指津」吗？
t_en: Welcome back, {NICKNAME}. Would you like another game of "Pathfinder"?

t_ch: 现在就玩。
t_en: Let's play!

t_ch: 我再准备下。
t_en: I need a moment to get ready.

t_ch: 我和宝箱在这等您。
t_en: I'll be waiting for you here with the chest.

t_ch: 我的玉兆在猛烈震动，这周围一定有「寻径指津」的高手！
t_en: My jade abacus is vibrating violently! A "Pathfinder" pro must be nearby!

t_ch: 正是在下。
t_en: That's me.

t_ch: 除了我，还有高手？
t_en: There's another pro besides me?

t_ch: 玉兆该修了。
t_en: Your jade abacus might be broken.

t_ch: 您一定是{NICKNAME}大人吧？我患有眼疾，看不见您，还请见谅。
t_en: You must be {NICKNAME}. My apologies, but I unfortunately can't see you because of an eye affliction.

t_ch: 不过我能从玉兆的震动中感受到您磅礴的棋力，不愧是我们「寻径指津」的最强棋手之一。
t_en: Through the vibrations of my jade abacus, however, I can tell that you must be a prodigious chess master. It's no wonder you're one of the best "Pathfinder" players.

t_ch: 你看不见还怎么下棋？
t_en: How do you play if you can't see?

t_ch: 帝弓为你关上一扇门，就会为你打开一扇窗。我虽然眼睛不好，但我耳朵特别灵，靠「听声辨位」也能下棋。
t_en: When the Reignbow closes a door, THEY open a window. My eyes may have failed, but my ears are particularly sharp. I can play simply by discerning the pieces' locations by sound.

t_ch: 你这玉兆是什么原理？
t_en: What's going on with your jade abacus?

t_ch: 这是一块植入了棋力检测程序的玉兆，说话的人棋力越强，它就震动的越厉害。您还是少说两句吧，我怕它震坏了。
t_en: This jade abacus is embedded with a program that can detect a person's chess proficiency when they speak. The higher the speaker's chess proficiency, the more violent its vibrations. That being so, I'd be grateful if you could hold your tongue a little — I'm afraid you'll break it.

t_ch: 我还不是最强的？
t_en: I'm not the best player?

t_ch: 这次还有一个从朱明来的棋手，实力与你难分高下。不过我相信只要您能解开我布置的棋局，那个「之一」就可以去掉了。
t_en: Regrettably, a player from the Zhuming whose skills are comparable to yours will also be participating this time. However, I'm confident that you'll take the top spot once you can solve this game I've set up.

t_ch: 我的话是不是有些太多了，要不我们直接开始下棋吧。
t_en: Ah, but I digress. Shall we start the game?

t_ch: 我准备好了。
t_en: I'm ready.

t_ch: 我再准备下。
t_en: I need a moment to get ready.

t_ch: 最强棋手的称号随时等待您摘取。
t_en: Sure. The title of Grandmaster will always be here for the taking.

t_ch: 准备好了么，我们继续下棋吧。
t_en: Are you ready? Let's resume playing.

t_ch: 现在就玩。
t_en: Let's play!

t_ch: 我再准备下。
t_en: I need a moment to get ready.

t_ch: 最强棋手的称号随时等待您摘取。
t_en: Sure. The title of Grandmaster will always be here for the taking.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}哪里走！快来与我大战八百回合，看谁真正有资格做「寻径指津」的意见领袖。
t_en: {NICKNAME}! Where do you think you're going? Come and face me! We'll see which one of us is more qualified to lead the "Pathfinder" community.

t_ch: 我没想走啊。
t_en: I wasn't going anywhere.

t_ch: 你吼那么大声干什么。
t_en: Why are you yelling?

t_ch: 你们圈子的人都喜欢一惊一乍？
t_en: Is everyone in the community this high-strung?

t_ch: 想我纵横「寻圈」三百年，打败的对手比掉落的头发还多，到头来却被你个小年轻抢了风头。
t_en: For three centuries have I dominated the Pathfinder community, trouncing more opponents than hairs have fallen off my scalp. Yet here you are, a young upstart trying to steal my thunder!

t_ch: 你以为侥幸赢了几盘棋，就有资格做我们「寻圈」的意见领袖？
t_en: Do you truly believe yourself qualified to lead the community just because you lucked out and won a few chess games?

t_ch: 哼！你给我记住，今天输给我以后，我不准你再玩「寻径指津」。
t_en: Hmph! You'd best remember this: If you lose to me today, you're forbidden from ever playing "Pathfinder" again.

t_ch: 那我要是赢了呢？
t_en: What if I win?

t_ch: 哈哈，这种事不可能发生。
t_en: Hah, that will never happen.

t_ch: 我还不稀罕玩呢。
t_en: I don't even want to play.

t_ch: 岂有此理，竟敢亵渎我们神圣的、伟大的、其乐无穷的「寻径指津」，今天我必让你输得彻彻底底。
t_en: Why, you little... You'd blaspheme against our greatest, most sacred, and most delightful "Pathfinder"!? Mark my words: I shall see you utterly defeated today!

t_ch: 为了宝箱，我必须赢你。
t_en: I'll win this game. I want that chest!

t_ch: 你居然为了这种庸俗的理由而下棋，你果然不配做我们的意见领袖。
t_en: You're playing for such a contemptible reason? I knew you weren't worthy of being our thought leader!

t_ch: 废话不多说，准备接受我的审判吧。
t_en: Alright, let's cut the chatter. Prepare yourself for judgment!

t_ch: 放马过来吧。
t_en: Bring it on.

t_ch: 我再准备下。
t_en: I need a moment to get ready.

t_ch: 你再怎么准备也不可能赢。
t_en: Hah, prepare all you want, but you'll never win.

t_ch: 这…怎么可能？这几盘棋凝聚了我三百年的棋力，竟然被你轻松化解了？
t_en: I—Impossible! Everything I've learned over three hundred years of chess playing, I've put into these puzzles, yet you solved them so easily!

t_ch: 三百年，就这？
t_en: Three hundred years, and that's the best you could do?

t_ch: 呵，你就嘲笑我吧！让我闭关修炼一番，咱们下个三百年再战！
t_en: Haha, go ahead and mock me! I'll go into seclusion for training. Let's battle again after three hundred years!

t_ch: 其实你的棋局设计的不错。
t_en: Those were some clever puzzles.

t_ch: 不用安慰我！让我闭关修炼一番，咱们下个三百年再战！
t_en: No need to comfort me! I'll go into seclusion for training. Let's battle again after three hundred years!

t_ch: 所以我的宝箱呢？
t_en: So, where is my chest?

t_ch: 拿着你的宝箱走吧。但你记住，三百年河东、三百年河西，莫欺少年穷！
t_en: Take your treasure chest and leave. But remember, fortunes change over three centuries, so don't underestimate the youth!

t_ch: 准备好了么，我们继续下棋吧。
t_en: Are you ready? Let's resume playing.

t_ch: 宝箱您先拿好，后面还有更多挑战等着您。
t_en: Here. Take this treasure for now. More challenges await.

t_ch: 宝箱归您了。相信以您的实力，下关挑战也不是问题。
t_en: The chest is yours. With your capabilities, the next challenge will likely pose no issue either.

t_ch: 你赖在这里是想羞辱我么？快拿着你的宝箱走开。
t_en: Are you trying to humiliate me by loitering around here? Take your chest and get out of here.

t_ch: 你有所不知，像我这样的还有四个。只有把我们四位都召集起来，「老大」才会出来。平时没什么事，它老人家不会轻易露面的。
t_en: You might not be aware, but there are four of us who look alike. Only when all four of us come together does the Boss deign to grace us with their presence. Otherwise, normally nobody gets to see them.

t_ch: 我们老大是个了不得的大人物，你想见见它吗？前提是…得通过我们四个的「考验」。
t_en: Our Boss is a real big shot. Would you like to meet them? But first, you'll need to pass the test posed by the four of us.

t_ch: …跟你说一个秘密，在这艘空艇上我还有三个好兄弟。你要是能找齐我们四个，我会把你引荐给我们的头儿。
t_en: ...Just between you and me, I have three buddies on this airship. If you can find all four of us, I'll introduce you to our Boss.

t_ch: 贵客，是你啊，谢谢你救了我。作为交换，有个秘密你要不要听？滴答！我和三个好朋友支撑起了空艇的半边天。你要是能把我们四个都修好，那你将见到剩下的那「半边天」。
t_en: Ah, it's you, our esteemed guest! Thank you for saving me! As a token of my gratitude, would you like to hear a secret? Tick-tock! My three buddies and I make half the contribution that keeps this airship running. If you can repair all four of us, you'll meet the one who contributes the other half.

t_ch: 哎，是你啊，尊敬的客人。你来得正好，我和我的几位同僚交流了一下——
t_en: Ah, it's you, our honored guest. You came at the right time. I've been speaking with my colleagues an—

t_ch: 什么同僚？咱们是空艇上「四大天王」，行不改名坐不改姓，名号可不能乱来。
t_en: Colleagues? We're the Formidable Four of the airship! Titles are not to be changed lightly!

t_ch: 去去去，都什么时候了，老大都成什么样了，还在纠结这些事，不像话！
t_en: Oh, hush now! Have you any inkling the gravity of our situation? With the Boss in their current state, now's no time to be fussing over titles!

t_ch: 什么是「四大天王」？
t_en: The Formidable Four?

t_ch: 我们四个加起来顶起了空艇的半片天，懂？我们是老大的手下，它老人家照看着整个空艇，还会抽空校准下我们。
t_en: We four make half the contribution that keeps this airship running, got it? We work for the Boss, who not only oversees the whole airship but also finds time to tweak our calibration while they're at it.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，等我们能自行运转起来后，就各自照看一个部门，共同维系空艇的秩序。
t_en: Uh-huh, once we're fully operational on our own, each of us will head a department and work together to keep the airship running smoothly.

t_ch: 别吵了，能不能说重点…
t_en: Enough chatter. Can we get to the point?

t_ch: 就是，没有老大哪有我们「四大天王」，赶紧说正事吧，以老大的安危为重！
t_en: Exactly! Without the Boss, there is no Formidable Four! The Boss's safety has to be our top priority!

t_ch: 简单来说，我们的老大受到不明重创倒下了，所以…之前我们几个就跟没了指针一样失魂落魄。
t_en: Long story short, for reasons unknown, our Boss became severely injured, and now they're down for the count. That's why we were so out of it earlier, as if we'd lost our hands.

t_ch: 我见你修好了我们四个…我就在想你能不能帮帮老大？我平时能处理刮刮碰碰，但这种大伤，我也只能看着干着急。
t_en: Now that you've fixed us four, do you think you could help our Boss as well? I can patch up scrapes and scratches, but I'm simply out of my depth with serious injuries like this.

t_ch: 是啊，虽然不知道客人你是怎么做到的，但我们想请你帮帮忙！
t_en: Yeah, we're not sure how exactly you pulled it off, but we really need your help!

t_ch: 好，我试试看。
t_en: Alright. I'll give it a shot.

t_ch: 麻烦阁下了！
t_en: Thank you so much!

t_ch: 你的能力真是神奇，居然能把我救回来！
t_en: Your skills are like magic! You actually fixed me!

t_ch: 其实我之前就注意到了，这种事时有发生，我去调查过，是一种奇怪的积木引起的。
t_en: Truth be told, I've noticed this issue — which happens from time to time — for a while now. When I looked into it, my findings revealed that it's caused by a strange type of building block.

t_ch: 这种积木散发着不祥的气息，时间长了，会干扰我们的心神，害我们故障频出……
t_en: These blocks emit an ominous aura, and over time, they interfere with our minds, causing us to malfunction...

t_ch: 只可惜…我还没找到积木就倒下了。不过，吃一堑长一智…现在我做足了全套防护工作，我不会再被它们影响了。
t_en: Unfortunately, I collapsed before I could find them. But we all live and learn, don't we? I've now taken full precautions to ensure that they won't affect me again.

t_ch: 就是不知道它们都藏在哪儿了…以我的能力，完全没有头绪。如果您手头有这种积木的话，可以交给我，把它们销毁掉是我的职责！
t_en: There's just a small problem: I don't know where they are anymore. With my limited abilities, I'm completely at a loss. If you happen to have any of these building blocks, please hand them over to me. It's my duty to destroy them!

t_ch: 很抱歉…根据我的推论，积木的数量应该不止这些…如果你手头还有积木，可以继续交到我这里。
t_en: I apologize, but my calculations indicate that there should be more building blocks than these. If you find any more, please bring them to me.

t_ch: 等我处理完，我会给你准备一份惊喜的，到时候记得来找我喔，好心人！
t_en: Once I've finished, I'll have a surprise for you. Be sure to come and see me, kind one!

t_ch: 真的太感谢了，你真是个好人！虽然报酬不多…不过这是大家的心意，还请你收下。
t_en: Thank you so much! You are too kind! It isn't much, but please take this. It's a token of our gratitude.

t_ch: …谢谢你，我感觉心里暖暖的，你之前送过来的积木，我已经处理完了，不会再对我的伙伴们产生负面影响了。
t_en: ...Thank you! I feel all warm and fuzzy inside now. I've already taken care of all the blocks you gave me. They won't harm my companions anymore.

t_ch: 我感觉心里有只鸟儿要飞出来了——飞翔…歌唱！
t_en: I feel like my inner bird is about to take flight and sing anytime!

t_ch: 表盘上的确有只鸟。
t_en: There is indeed a bird on the dial.

t_ch: …这不是你的幻觉。
t_en: Your mind's not playing tricks on you.

t_ch: 我感觉我的齿轮有些不一样了…表盘里有一种东西在萌芽…我好想去这个世界看看。
t_en: I feel my gears transforming... Something is starting to sprout on the dial... I can't wait to see the world!

t_ch: …或许这和你的慷慨有关，我要开启我的旅途了，保重！
t_en: ...Perhaps your kindness is what set all this in motion. It's time for my journey to start. Stay well!

t_ch: 你想见见我们老大吗？
t_en: Would you like to meet our boss?

t_ch: …我感觉老大还能抢救一下。
t_en: ...I'm sure it's not too late for the boss.

t_ch: {F#她}{M#他}来了……
t_en: {F#She}{M#He} is here...

t_ch: 贵客，麻烦您帮个忙…
t_en: A favor if I may, dear esteemed guest...

t_ch: 怪梦积木请交到我这里来！
t_en: Please hand over Eerie Building Blocks to me!

t_ch: 喔！你来了呀。
t_en: Oh! You've arrived.

t_ch: 你好，我是「枘凿六合」秘密结社的「主智囊」。
t_en: Hello, I am the "Main Think-Tank" for the Hexanexus secret society.

t_ch: 既然都被关起来了，这里也不必遵守隐秘组织的要求，以代号称呼彼此…我这名字也告诉您无妨，我叫西桥，被抓之前一直是工造司的匠人。
t_en: Since I'm already locked up, there's hardly any reason to adhere to the secret society's requirements and address one another by our aliases... I see no harm in divulging my name. I'm Seymour, formerly a craftsman within the Artisanship Commission before my arrest.

t_ch: 我知道你有很多问题想问我，你问吧，问完我还有事得拜托你……
t_en: I understand you have lots of questions for me. Go ahead, and I'll have a favor to ask in return...

t_ch: 要怎样做游戏才会被抓进牢里？
t_en: How do you get arrested and sent to jail for developing games?

t_ch: 「枘凿六合」出了些事情…要我自己来讲实在是有些难以启齿啊……
t_en: Something happened to Hexanexus... It's a little hard for me to explain it on my own...

t_ch: 您知道私服吗？
t_en: Do you know what's a private server?

t_ch: 我知道什么是私服…
t_en: Yeah, I do...

t_ch: 那解释起来就方便了。
t_en: In that case, it simplifies the explanation.

t_ch: 我只知道什么是师傅。
t_en: Like a personal butler?

t_ch: 不是师傅，是私服。就是说，私自架设服务器，是对知识产权危害最大的犯罪行为之一。
t_en: Not that kind of server. I meant a computer that shares information with other computers. In other words, setting up a server privately ranks among the gravest offenses against intellectual property.

t_ch: 所以，你是架设私服…
t_en: So, you set up a private server...

t_ch: 不不不，您误会了！我把架设私服的人找了出来，然后以暴制暴……
t_en: No, don't just jump the gun! I found the culprits responsible for making the private server and punished them through violent means...

t_ch: 然后他们地衡司说我的威胁程度比较大，还是关幽囚狱比较好，就联系了判官。
t_en: Later, the Realm-Keeping Commission declared that my threat level was high and it would be safer to lock me up in The Shackling Prison, so they contacted the judges.

t_ch: 所以，就像您看到的一样，我也被抓起来了…不过你放心，那个架设私服的判得更重！十王司对私服绝不姑息！
t_en: In the end, as you can see, I was apprehended too... But don't worry, that private server people were punished even harder! The Ten-Lords Commission will never tolerate private servers!

t_ch: 你在牢里也能进行游戏开发？
t_en: Can you also develop games behind bars?

t_ch: 在哪里开发不是开发呢？在工造司的工位上，在丹鼎司的病房里，在十王司的牢房里，其实开发环境都差不多的。
t_en: Why not? Whether in the workplace of the Artisanship Commission, the hospital ward of the Alchemy Commission, or the cell of the Ten-Lords Commission, the environment for game development is similar.

t_ch: 在牢里开发不需要和别人开那些没完没了的会，也不用加班，自己想做就做，想休息就休息，也没有什么死线压力，真是太美妙了。
t_en: In fact, it's oddly liberating to develop games in prison. No more endless meetings or overtime. I have the freedom to develop at my own pace, taking breaks whenever necessary, and being free from the relentless pressure of meeting deadlines.

t_ch: 当然缺点也是显而易见的——我快要自闭了。
t_en: Of course, the downside is evident — I'm on the verge of becoming socially withdrawn.

t_ch: 为什么要做「Remake」？
t_en: Why a "Remake"?

t_ch: 以「造型优美的魔方」为契机，最近有许多玩家对「枘凿六合」系列产生了兴趣。
t_en: Taking the allure of the "beautifully shaped cube" as an opportunity, many gamers have recently become interested in Hexanexus.

t_ch: 为了让不熟悉「枘凿六合」的玩家了解到本系列之原点，作为系列一周年重要作品之一，我们选择了对「枘凿六合」进行重制。
t_en: To acquaint gamers unfamiliar with Hexanexus with the series' origin, we decided to remake and release the game as a cornerstone of the series' first anniversary celebrations.

t_ch: 「枘凿六合」是系列作品？
t_en: Hexanexus a serial work?

t_ch: 有成为系列作品的打算，目前只有一作。
t_en: There are plans to make it a series. We have only one game so far.

t_ch: 真的有很多玩家感兴趣吗？
t_en: Are there really that many gamers interested in it?

t_ch: 有的有的，我身边的人都玩。
t_en: Yes, of course. Everyone around me is playing it.

t_ch: 重做之后，我们考虑过副标题要用「Remake」、「Rebirth」、「Remastered」还是「Return」、「Recall」……
t_en: After the remake, we considered whether to use the terms "Remake," "Rebirth," "Remastered," "Return," or "Recall" in our subheading...

t_ch: 甚至还有激进一些的方案，叫作「Re凿六合」。
t_en: There was even a more radical option called "Re-xanexus."

t_ch: 别「Re」了…
t_en: Sounds re-diculous...

t_ch: 我们看市面上流行的作品都是这样的。
t_en: All the popular game titles on the market adopt the same strategy.

t_ch: 最后我们还是决定使用了简单易懂的「Remake」。
t_en: Finally, we decided to take the simplest approach and chose "Remake."

t_ch: 你就直说吧，什么事情？
t_en: Let's cut the chase. What do you need?

t_ch: 多亏了你，「枘凿六合•Remake」的前6题测试完成了。过些天，我会委托十王司的冥差们帮我把幽囚狱谜题刷新一遍，会有一些新的谜题。
t_en: Thanks to you, the first six tests of the "Hexanexus: Remake" are completed. In a few days, I'll assign the spiritfarers in the Ten-Lords Commission to refresh The Shackling Prison puzzles, and new puzzles will be available.

t_ch: 不过你知道吗，除了你以外，真的没有人在玩……
t_en: But let's be frank, aside from yourself, there's hardly anyone playing it...

t_ch: 这很合理，因为这里是幽囚狱…
t_en: Fair enough. After all, this is The Shackling Prison...

t_ch: 这很合理，因为魔方不太好玩…
t_en: Fair enough. After all, Hexanexus isn't that fun...

t_ch: 我想，除了你也没有人会玩新的一轮谜题了。所以，请你一定要把测试版的内容玩完啊！
t_en: Thus, I doubt anyone besides yourself will be playing the new round of puzzles. So, please make sure you complete the beta!

t_ch: 你放心，你解开谜题后都会得到「魔方战令」，这是我们新推出一项「阶段性奖励服务」，你带着它来找我，我就可以给你奖励，简单吧？
t_en: Don't worry. Upon completing the puzzles, you will obtain Hex Passes. This is a new type of Segmented Reward Service. Bring the Hex Passes to me, and I'll reward you. Simple?

t_ch: 这么复杂的吗？
t_en: Why is it so complicated?

t_ch: 我每个版本都买。
t_en: I purchased every version of it.

t_ch: 我哪也去不了，就在这里等你的好消息了。
t_en: I'm not going anywhere. I'll wait here for your good news.

t_ch: 你好，这里是「枘凿六合•Remake」第四开发部。请问有什么事？
t_en: Hi, this is Department IV of the "Hexanexus: Remake" Developers. How may I help you?

t_ch: 我带着新鲜的战令来啦。
t_en: I'm here with new Hex Passes.

t_ch: 恭喜您在「枘凿六合•Remake」测试活动中获得以下奖励，请注意查收。
t_en: Congratulations! You've obtained the following rewards from the "Hexanexus: Remake" beta. You may check them in your inventory.

t_ch: 找你聊会天…
t_en: I'm here to have a chat with you...

t_ch: 想聊些什么？
t_en: What do you want to talk about?

t_ch: 要怎样做游戏才会被抓进牢里？
t_en: How do you get arrested and sent to jail for developing games?

t_ch: 「枘凿六合」出了些事情…要我自己来讲实在是有些难以启齿啊……
t_en: Something happened to Hexanexus... It's a little hard for me to explain it on my own...

t_ch: 我把架设「枘凿六合」私服的人找了出来，然后以暴制暴。
t_en: I found the culprits responsible for the Hexanexus private server and punished them through violent means.

t_ch: 你在牢里也能进行游戏开发？
t_en: Can you also develop games behind bars?

t_ch: 在哪里开发不是开发呢？在工造司的工位上，在丹鼎司的病房里，在十王司的牢房里，其实开发环境都差不多的。
t_en: Why not? Whether in the workplace of the Artisanship Commission, the hospital ward of the Alchemy Commission, or the cell of the Ten-Lords Commission, the environment for game development is similar.

t_ch: 在牢里开发不需要和别人开那些没完没了的会，也不用加班，自己想做就想休息就休息，也没有什么死线压力，真是太美妙了。
t_en: In fact, it's oddly liberating to develop games in prison. No more endless meetings or overtime. I have the freedom to develop at my own pace, taking breaks whenever necessary, and being free from the relentless pressure of meeting deadlines.

t_ch: 当然缺点也是显而易见的——我快要自闭了。
t_en: Of course, the downside is evident — I'm on the verge of becoming socially withdrawn.

t_ch: 为什么要做「Remake」？
t_en: Why a "Remake"?

t_ch: 以「造型优美的魔方」为契机，最近有许多玩家对「枘凿六合」系列产生了兴趣。
t_en: Taking the allure of the "beautifully shaped cube" as an opportunity, many gamers have recently become interested in Hexanexus.

t_ch: 为了让不熟悉「枘凿六合」的玩家了解到本系列之原点，作为系列一周年重要作品之一，我们选择了对「枘凿六合」进行重制。
t_en: To acquaint gamers unfamiliar with Hexanexus with the series' origin, we decided to remake and release the game as a cornerstone of the series' first anniversary celebrations.

t_ch: 重做之后，我们考虑过副标题要用「Remake」、「Rebirth」、「Remastered」还是「Return」、「Recall」……
t_en: After the remake, we considered whether to use the terms "Remake," "Rebirth," "Remastered," "Return," or "Recall" in our subheading...

t_ch: 最后还是决定使用了简单易懂的「Remake」。
t_en: Finally, we decided to take the simplest approach and chose "Remake."

t_ch: 十王司同意你随处乱放魔方？
t_en: Did the Ten-Lords Commission approve of you leaving Hexanexus lying around?

t_ch: 说起来我也感觉到很意外，毕竟这里是幽囚狱嘛。
t_en: I'm also surprised. After all, it's The Shackling Prison.

t_ch: 但是雪衣判官说，只要把这个魔方放在路边，就能硬控逃犯半个小时，降低了幽囚狱的出逃成功率，是一个可以尝试的方案。
t_en: However, Judge Xueyi suggested that strategically placing this Hexanexus on the side of the road could temporarily incapacitate the fugitive for up to half an hour, significantly lowering the chances of successful escape from The Shackling Prison. It's certainly worth a try.

t_ch: 于是我们达成了一致。我提供魔方的谜题，他们可以帮我摆放在幽囚狱里。
t_en: So we came to a consensus. I would provide the puzzles for the Hexanexus, and they could place them in The Shackling Prison for me.

t_ch: 我没什么事了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 请记住「枘凿六合•Remake」天下第一！我先去忙去了。
t_en: Just remember that "Hexanexus: Remake" is the world's number one game! I should get to work now.

t_ch: 我没什么事了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 请记住「枘凿六合•Remake」天下第一！我先去忙去了。
t_en: Just remember that Hexanexus: Remake is the world's number one game! I should get to work now.

t_ch: 怎么幽囚狱里面也有「枘凿六合」？
t_en: Why is Hexanexus also available in The Shackling Prison?

t_ch: 推广游戏都推广到监狱里了…魔方秘社的人这么狂的吗？
t_en: To think they even promote their game in the prison... Are the members of the Hex Club obsessed with it?

t_ch: 这里应该就是「魔方秘社」所在的囚室了……
t_en: This must be the cell where the Hex Club is...

t_ch: 上前去问问吧。
t_en: Let's go up and ask.

t_ch: 哦哦哦哦！新的谜题你都完成了，我这里暂时没有什么新的谜题了。
t_en: Ohhhh! You've completed all the new puzzles! I don't have any more new puzzles at the moment.

t_ch: …您这是不是算玩得挺投入的？
t_en: ...Can I consider you as a hardcore fan of this game?

t_ch: 挺好的，还想接着玩。
t_en: The game is pretty fun, and I would like to play it again.

t_ch: 还行，比坐牢好一些。
t_en: The game is fine. At least it's better than doing nothing in jail.

t_ch: 您放心，我已经给秘密结社的其他成员通过气了。我们「枘凿六合•Remake」即将登录竞锋舰平台，成为跨平台开发游戏！保证是全新的版本，全新的体验。
t_en: Fret not. I've discussed this with the other members of the Hex Club. Our "Hexanexus: Remake" will soon be accessible on the Skysplitter platform as a cross-platform game! It's set to be a completely revamped version, promising gamers an entirely fresh experience.

t_ch: 你们简直是手眼通天。
t_en: You people truly have connections everywhere.

t_ch: 只能说您不想玩，可千万不能说您玩不到。
t_en: Unless you're not keen to play, there's simply no reason you can't play it.

t_ch: 还我一个干净的竞锋舰。
t_en: I'd rather have a clean Skysplitter.

t_ch: 我们的魔方都是经过灭菌杀毒处理过的，请不必担心。
t_en: Our Hexanexus are sterilized and treated with anti-virus, so please do not worry.

t_ch: 那么，就敬请期待了。
t_en: That's all. Stay tuned!

t_ch: 滴、答…游客，我有一个问题。
t_en: Tick, tock... Guest, I have a question.

t_ch: 假设，只是假设…你接下来要玩个游戏，一个使用视差错觉来设置难题的解谜游戏。
t_en: Hypothetically, just hypothetically... if you were playing a game straight after this, a puzzle game designed using parallax illusions.

t_ch: 你觉得，什么难度能给你带来最大的乐趣？
t_en: What level of difficulty do you think would bring you the most enjoyment?

t_ch: 奖励有区别吗？
t_en: Is there a difference in reward?

t_ch: 没有，乐趣才是重点。
t_en: No. Enjoyment is the key.

t_ch: 还能选难度？
t_en: You can choose difficulties?

t_ch: 都说是假设…算了，确实能选难度。你随便说。
t_en: I already said it was hypothetical... Forget it. Yes, you can choose your difficulty level. Just say whatever you want.

t_ch: 戳一下就过关的那种！
t_en: The level where you can clear the stage with a quick press!

t_ch: 当然，最为稳妥的选项。我欣赏你，因为我也会选一样的路。
t_en: Of course, that is the optimal choice. I admire you, because I would make the same choice.

t_ch: 普通的就好。
t_en: Just normal will do.

t_ch: 大部分情况，人们往往倾向于更加极端的选择，你很…与众不同。
t_en: In most situations, people typically lean towards the more extreme choice. You are... different.

t_ch: 让我见识下真正的谜题！
t_en: Show me a real puzzle!

t_ch: 滴答…对，没错…理应如此…我会满足你的要求，游客。我会全力以赴。
t_en: Tick-tock... Yes, that's right... So it shall be... I will fulfill your request, guest. I will do my best.

t_ch: 你想要的难度设置好了。上吧。
t_en: The difficulty you asked for has been configured. Go forth.

t_ch: 别忘了，如果不满意，在完成前再来找我就行，我会在这等着。
t_en: Don't forget. If you are dissatisfied, you can just talk to me before completing it. I will be here waiting.

t_ch: 进入心境空间。
t_en: Enter the Emoscape.

t_ch: 滴、答…游客，我们又见面了。
t_en: Tick, tock... We meet again, guest.

t_ch: 我猜是因为好奇心？你明明可以立刻通过，但却没有选择这么做。
t_en: I'm guessing because of curiosity? You could have cleared it with ease, but yet did not choose to do so.

t_ch: 无论如何，很高兴能再见到你，试试其他难度吗？
t_en: No matter what, I am happy to see you again. Care to try other difficulties?

t_ch: 刚才的就好。
t_en: Just the previous one will do.

t_ch: 我想换成*普通难度*。
t_en: I'd like to change to *Normal Difficulty*.

t_ch: 我想换成*挑战难度*。
t_en: I'd like to change to *Challenging Difficulty*.

t_ch: 没问题，上吧。
t_en: No problem. Go.

t_ch: 进入心境空间。
t_en: Enter the Emoscape.

t_ch: 滴、答…游客，我们又见面了。
t_en: Tick, tock... We meet again, guest.

t_ch: 哈哈，是不是有点普通？但能与你相遇，我和你就都已经不再平凡了。
t_en: Haha, was it a little too normal? But you and I are no longer normal, for I have met you.

t_ch: 总之，欢迎回来。试试其他难度吗？
t_en: In any case, welcome back. Care to try other difficulties?

t_ch: 刚刚的就好。
t_en: Just the previous one will do.
